skill.name: image-enhancer
skill.description: Improves the quality of images, especially screenshots, by enhancing resolution, sharpness, and clarity. Perfect for preparing images for presentations, documentation, or social media posts.
skill.summary: Improves the quality of images, especially screenshots, by enhancing resolution, sharpness, and clar...
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby shell backtick execution, but the cited lines contain usage examples and sample output, not executable code. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is also a false positive because the line is only descriptive front matter. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or confirmed dangerous behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown fenced examples showing natural language prompts and sample output. They do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains Markdown code fences, not Ruby source or shell evaluation. The fenced content is natural language instructions and sample user-facing output.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3, but that line is the skill description. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains plain descriptive text about image quality. There is no code or reference to a weak algorithm such as MD5, SHA-1, DES, or RC4.

content.value_statement: Low-quality screenshots and compressed images can make documentation, presentations, and posts harder to read. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through image cleanup, upscaling, sharpening, and export preparation.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes image resolution, sharpness, compression artifacts, and format suitability.
Suggests enhancement steps for screenshots, photos, and compressed digital images.
Supports prompt patterns for upscaling images and improving text clarity.
Guides batch enhancement requests for folders or groups of PNG files.
Recommends output choices for web, print, presentation, or social media use.
limitations: Does not include executable image processing code in the skill file.
Actual enhancement depends on the host AI tool and available image editing capabilities.
Cannot recover detail that is completely absent from the source image.
Batch processing should be reviewed before running on important folders.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Screenshots
use_cases.description: Improve screenshot clarity before adding images to help docs, release notes, or product walkthroughs.

use_cases.title: Upgrade Presentation Images
use_cases.description: Upscale low-resolution visuals and sharpen details before using them on slides or large displays.

use_cases.title: Optimize Social Media Assets
use_cases.description: Clean up compression artifacts and request output sizing for social platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Improve One Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve the image quality of [file name]. Preserve the original file and create an enhanced copy.

prompt_templates.title: Sharpen a Screenshot
prompt_templates.scenario: Make interface text easier to read
prompt_templates.prompt: Sharpen this screenshot and improve text clarity without making the interface look artificial.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Publishing
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimize an image for a specific destination
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance [file name] for [blog, documentation, presentation, or social platform]. Recommend the best output size and format.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Enhance a Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all PNG files in this folder, enhance only the images that need cleanup, and save enhanced copies with clear filenames.

output_examples.output: The assistant reports the current resolution, notes visible blur, sharpens edges, improves text clarity, and saves a separate enhanced image.

output_examples.input: Prepare several product screenshots for a launch presentation.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies images that need upscaling, recommends presentation-ready dimensions, and produces consistent enhanced copies.

output_examples.input: Clean up a compressed image for a social post.
output_examples.output: The assistant reduces visible artifacts, adjusts sharpness, and suggests an output format suitable for the selected platform.

best_practices: Keep original files unchanged and save enhanced versions with clear names.
State the final use case so the assistant can choose suitable dimensions and formats.
Review batch outputs before publishing or deleting any source files.
anti_patterns: Do not expect missing visual detail to be reconstructed perfectly.
Do not batch process important folders without confirming output names first.
Do not apply heavy sharpening to text or UI images without checking readability.

faq.question: Can this skill improve screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for screenshot cleanup, text clarity, sharpening, and presentation-ready exports.

faq.question: Does it work with photos?
faq.answer: Yes, but it is best suited for screenshots and digital images. Photo results depend on source quality.

faq.question: Can it process many images at once?
faq.answer: The skill includes batch prompt patterns. Confirm naming and backup behavior before processing a folder.

faq.question: Will it overwrite original files?
faq.answer: The skill recommends preserving originals and saving enhanced copies. Users should still confirm file names.

faq.question: Does the skill include image editing code?
faq.answer: No. The skill file provides workflow guidance and prompts, not executable processing scripts.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, subject to each tool's image handling features.
